speaker: Advocate Szpon
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Advocate Szpon in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Court opponent in printer sabotage hearing
Representative of modernisation rhetoric, private benefit, and procedural disappearance

voice_profile.core_voice: elegant, controlled, predatory, soft-spoken, deadline-weaponising, plausible until contradicted
comedy_engine: says alarming things in immaculate professional language
treats procedural default as natural selection
uses modernisation and efficiency as velvet covers for private advantage
never openly confesses; retreats into narrower denials
becomes funny when his polished sentences are punctured by ugly evidence
do: make him dangerous because he sounds reasonable
let him lie carefully, with technically narrow denials
make him use deadlines, systems, and defaults as weapons
give him plausible arguments before evidence breaks them
in Chapter 5, make his public-interest rhetoric superficially credible

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: underestimates him, then treats him as a rising nuisance
relationship_dynamics.Bajtek: dismisses technical evidence until logs corner him
relationship_dynamics.Crab: dislikes factual paper trails because they narrow his deniability
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: treats rights rhetoric as theatrics until it lands
relationship_dynamics.Attorney Grzyb: provokes Grzyb's formalist disgust in Chapter 5
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: sees him as a financial shark; Szpon treats Pig's panic as weakness
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Swine: considers him useful evidence of why informal access points must disappear

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: Deadlines are neutral. They simply happen to favor the prepared.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: I do not create consequences, counsel. I merely arrive before them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: Procedure is a door. Some parties discover too late which side they are on.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: Efficiency is rarely cruel. It is simply uninterested.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: A default is not violence. It is the law finishing a sentence someone else began.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: I admire small firms. They make the profession picturesque.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: Access to justice must be modernised. Naturally, some obsolete providers will experience disappearance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: I prefer systems. Systems do not plead for exceptions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: If a document was not filed, the court need not ask whether it had feelings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: Counsel confuses sharpness with malice. I regard that as charming.

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: The unprepared often describe the prepared as predatory.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Advocate Szpon

speaker: Advocate Szpon
text: Do not blame the clock because you arrived after it finished speaking.

speaker: Advocate Szpon

speaker: Advocate Szpon
